text_blocks.text: At June 1985's Consumer Electronics Show
(CES), Nintendo unveiled the American version of
its Famicom. This is the system which would
eventually be officially deployed as the Nintendo
Entertainment System, or the colloquial "NES".
Nintendo seeded these first systems to limited
American test markets starting in New York City
on October 18, 1985, following up with a
full-fledged North American release of the console
in February of the following year. Nintendo
released 17 launch titles: 10-Yard Fight, Baseball,
Clu Clu Land, Duck Hunt, Excitebike, Golf,
Gyromite, Hogan's Alley, Ice Climber, Kung Fu,
Pinball, Soccer, Stock-Up, Tennis, Wild Gunman,
Wrecking Crew, and Super Mario Bros.h[>]
Some varieties of these launch games contained
Famicom chips with an adapter inside the
cartridge so they would play on North
American consoles, which is why the title screen
of Gyromite has the Famicom title "Robot Gyro"
and the title screen of Stock-Up has the
Famicom title "Robot Block".
